data.title: Crude oil drops to YTD low as OPEC expected to delay planned production hike
data.description:
Crude oil futures closed Friday at their lowest settlement price this year, with both WTI and Brent benchmarks tumbling more than $2/bbl, fueled by near-term supply concerns and expectations that OPEC+ will postpone returning barrels to the market in April as planned.

Oil traders and analysts expect OPEC+ will again delay plans to revive oil production, despite pressure from President Trump, as global markets contend with a potential surplus; a postponement of the group's planned 120K bbl/day output increase would mark the fourth delay in plans to revive production that was first halted in 2022.

"The downside for prices is high if OPEC proceeds with production hikes," ANZ Research wrote, as the cartel and its allies face potential challenges from shifting U.S. policy and risks of a trade war, while renewed maximum pressure on Iran, sanctions on Russia and the halt of Venezuelan crude oil purchases add to the complexity, and rising fiscal pressures among OPEC members adds to their focus on maximizing revenue.

"Given prices in the mid-$70s, we continue to anticipate that [OPEC+] postpones the beginning of bringing back withheld oil supply to market," according to Citigroup analysts, adding that a decision to bring back more oil to market "might only come if the U.S. exerts more sanctions pressure on Iran amid potential negotiations."

Reuters reported the Trump administration is piling pressure on Iraq to allow oil exports from the Kurdistan region to restart or face sanctions, as a speedy resumption of exports that has cut flows of more than 300K bbl/day for nearly two years would help offset a potential drop in Iranian oil exports, which Trump has pledged to cut to zero.

Analysts said the relative calm in the Middle East as the Gaza ceasefire held has reduced risk in the market, and pointed to media reports indicating that researchers at the Wuhan Institute of Virology in China said they discovered a new coronavirus in bats.

Investors also continued to evaluate another increase in U.S. crude oil inventories, reported on Thursday, as seasonal maintenance at refineries led to lower processing, the Energy Information Administration said.

Traders also are watching U.S. efforts for talks to end the Russia-Ukraine war, as "an array of geopolitical, military and macroeconomic crosscurrents continues to limit upside follow-through in WTI to above the $73.50 level while restricting downside to the $70 area," Ritterbusch wrote.

Russia said oil flows from the Caspian Pipeline Consortium, a major route for crude exports from Kazakhstan through Russia, were cut by 30%-40% earlier this week after a Ukrainian drone attack on a pumping station, but industry sources told Reuters that Kazakhstan is pumping record high oil volumes despite the damage to the CPC export route.

Friday's losses pushed both major crude oil benchmarks into the red for the week, with front-month April Nymex crude (CL1:COM) and April Brent crude (CO1:COM) both finishing -0.4% to $70.40/bbl and $74.43/bbl, respectively; on Friday, WTI fell 2.9% and Brent dropped 2.7%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

U.S. natural gas futures ended +13.6% for the week as an Arctic blast lifted heating demand across much of the country, while deliveries to liquefied natural gas export terminals hit new highs above 16B cf/day; front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for March delivery gained 2% on Friday to $4.234/MMBtu.

Cheniere Energy's announcement this week of a first cargo from its Corpus Christi Stage 3 expansion was "big news for LNG," Gelber & Associates said, and while milder temperatures next week are expected to reduce natural gas demand, and production is seen picking up after freeze-offs, prices should find support in the growing storage deficit given this winter’s hefty withdrawals.

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE), ended the week +0.9%.

Top 5 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Energy Focus (EFOI) +65.3%, Solaris Energy Infrastructure (SEI) +31.9%, SolarEdge Technologies (SEDG) +18.6%, San Juan Basin Royalty Trust (SJT) +15.6%, Obsidian Energy (OBE) +12.8%.

Top 10 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Critical Minerals (CRML) -41.8%, Perpetua Resources (PPTA) -30.2%, Oklo (OKLO) -29.5%, ESS Tech (GWH) -26.2%, Lightbridge (LTBR) -25.5%, Coeur Mining (CDE) -24.4%, Nuscale Power (SMR) -22.9%, Hudbay Minerals (HBM) -21.7%, Uranium Energy (UEC) -18.6%, Hecla Mining (HL) -18%.

Source: Barchart.com

data.title: Yoshiharu receives delisting notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Yoshiharu Global Co. (NASDAQ:YOSH) on Friday announced that it has received a notice of delisting from Nasdaq because it remains out of compliance with listing rules, which requires that the company maintain stockholders’ equity of at least $2.5Mfor continued listing.
  • The company intends to request a hearing to appeal the determination.
  • YOSH +11.98% after hours to $9.81.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Gold futures gain for eighth straight week on safe-haven demand, central bank buying
data.description:
Gold futures edged lower Friday but finished the week higher, marking the yellow metal's eighth consecutive week of gains - the longest stretch of weekly wins since August 2020, when the streak lasted nine weeks.

Front-month futures have shattered record highs this week, setting a fresh intraday high of $2,973.40/oz before ending Thursday at an all-time high settlement of $2,940/oz, as uncertainties surrounding global economic growth and political instability have underscored investor appetite for safe havens such as gold, which has jumped 11.7% YTD and 44.1% in the past year.

"Demand for gold is currently being driven primarily by western investors and central banks. ETF investors appear to be jumping on the bandwagon," Commerzbank analysts wrote.

The continued strong appetite for gold comes mostly from central banks, according to Catalyst Funds analyst David Miller: "The biggest factor driving gold to $3,000/oz is massive coordinated buying by BRICS central banks."

A test of $3,000 is starting to look like a question of "when" rather than "if," Pepperstone's Michael Brown said, noting the mark might give the bulls a point to reassess gold's momentum and perhaps take some profit, but upward momentum should continue on safe-haven demand supported by President Trump's tariff threats, lingering trade and economic uncertainty, and persistent geopolitical tensions.

If Trump takes a more aggressive approach to tariffs, raising inflation risks, gold can be viewed as a debasement hedge, and in a more benign scenario, gold would still benefit from falling yields and higher interest rate cut expectations, MUFG analysts said.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for February delivery ended the week +1.9% to $2,937.60/oz, while front-month February silver (XAGUSD:CUR) closed +0.5% this week at $32.976/oz; on Friday, gold edged 0.1% lower and silver slipped 1.4%.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

President Trump said this week he would "make sure the gold is there when asked about concerns over the Fort Knox gold supply, and new Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent mentioned monetizing the U.S. balance sheet, but Bessent dismissed revaluing U.S. gold reserves and ruled out gold as an asset for a new U.S. sovereign wealth fund, SP Angel said.

data.title: Ynvisible Interactive announces $600,000 private placement
data.description:
• Ynvisible Interactive (TSXV:YNV:CA) on Friday said it has arranged a non-brokered private placement financing of up to 5 million common shares of the company at $0.12 per share for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $600,000.
  • The company said proceeds from the private Placement will be used as working capital and for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Rumble files for stock offering
data.description:

  • SEC Filing

data.title: Imaflex announces cybersecurity incident
data.description:
• Imaflex (TSXV:IFX:CA) on Friday announced that its operations have been affected by a cybersecurity incident on certain of the corporation's systems.
  • The company said it immediately took steps to contain and mitigate any potential impact on the corporation's data and operations and has launched a comprehensive investigation to fully ascertain the source and the extent of the incident and is working closely with third-party cybersecurity experts to support these efforts in accordance with industry best practices.
  • Although Imaflex's operations have been impacted, the company continues to manufacture, ship and perform back-office functions consistent with business requirements, albeit with some temporary workarounds.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cross Country Healthcare/Aya get 2nd request from FTC on deal
data.description:
Cross Country Healthcare (NASDAQ:CCRN) said that the company received a second request from the Federal Trade Commission regarding the travel nursing company's planned sale to Aya Healthcare. Cross Country fell 3.3% in after hours trading.

The request for more information extends the waiting period under the HSR act until 30 days after both the company and Aya substantially comply with the 2nd request, according to the 8-K filing.Cross Country (NASDAQ:CCRN) and Aya have been working cooperatively with the FTC and will continue to do so. CCRN now expects the deal will close in the second half of the year.

The latest filing comes after Cross Country and Aya last month said its HSR filing was pulled and refiled for the transaction. The HSR waiting period expired on Thursday.

Aya Healthcare in December entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Cross Country for $18.61/share in cash.

data.title: SaverOne implements change in ratio of shares to ADSs to meet Nasdaq minimum bid price rule
data.description:
• SaverOne 2014 (NASDAQ:SVRE) on Friday said it has received a letter from Nasdaq, notifying the company that it was not in compliance with listing rules, which requires listed companies to maintain a minimum bid price of $1.00 per share.
  • The company said it is not eligible for any compliance period as it has effected a reverse stock split over the prior one-year period. The company’s ADSs are subject to delisting from Nasdaq unless it requests an appeal of this determination by February 27, 2025.
  • The company’s ADSs will continue to trade on Nasdaq under the symbol “SVRE” pending the hearing and the Panel’s decision.
  • The company implemented an additional change in the ratio of its ADS to ordinary shares and at the close of market on February 21, 2025 the closing bid price for its ADS was over $1.00.
  • The company intends to provide the panel with a plan for compliance with the Minimum Bid Price Requirement.
  • SVRE +5.1% after hours to $4.32.
  • Source: Press Release
